hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of thermodynamically validated synthesis trajectories—generated by running the CALPHAD-JMA simulator across exhaustive combinations of precursor additions, temperatures, and wait times—will produce a domain-adapted model whose success rate on multi-phase synthesis challenges (analogous to Challenges 2 and 3) significantly exceeds that of the prompt-tuned GPT-4o baseline, because replacing implicit pretraining priors with explicit, system-specific thermodynamic knowledge will reduce the frequency of goal-temperature neglect errors (as observed in the unsuccessful Challenge 4 attempt) and improve phase-fraction accuracy in cases requiring non-adjacent phase traversal.
